title: derris guilleminiana
intro: Derris guilleminiana, also known as the Guillemin's derris or Derris, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a fish poison, but it also possesses various health benefits. The plant contains rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the root and stem bark of Derris guilleminiana are often used to make infusions, tinctures, or decoctions that are said to offer relief from various health issues.
